review_summary.one_line: Services supports device retention, while capital checks indicate an asset-light model with normal reinvestment constraints.
highlights: One synergy is forecast-eligible but capped because causality is partial.
Capital intensity is low relative to revenue, supporting an asset-light exemption.
warnings: Installed-base monetization should not be treated as proof of unlimited pricing power.

sources.name: Apple FY2025 Form 10-K
sources.excerpt: Services revenue includes advertising, AppleCare, cloud, digital content and payment services.

sources.name: Apple FY2025 Form 10-K
sources.excerpt: Payments for acquisition of property, plant and equipment were disclosed in investing activities.

claims.text: Apple Services monetizes the installed base created by iPhone and other devices.
claims.source_snippet: Services includes cloud, digital content, payment and AppleCare offerings.

claims.text: Apple capital spending is modest relative to revenue, so Step 5 does not need a hard reinvestment ceiling.
claims.source_snippet: PP&E purchases are disclosed in the cash flow statement.

synergy_registry.mechanism: Device ownership creates default access points for App Store, iCloud, AppleCare and payments.
synergy_registry.product_impact: Services can bundle and distribute through native device experiences.
synergy_registry.competitor_constraint: Standalone service competitors lack operating-system level distribution on Apple devices.

financial_signal.evidence: Services revenue is disclosed separately and linked to offerings consumed by the installed base.

flywheel.loop_description: Installed base -> services adoption -> ecosystem switching cost -> device retention

synergy_registry.financial_metric_link: Services revenue growth and gross margin
synergy_registry.review_rationale: The relationship passes integration but causality remains capped because service growth is not solely caused by device sales.

capital_metrics.objective: Maintain product tooling, data centers and operational capacity.
capital_metrics.strategic_leverage: Supports ecosystem reliability without requiring a revenue ceiling.
capital_metrics.review_note: Uses PP&E purchase line rather than total investing cash flow.

feasibility_checkpoints.capex_runway: Normal: historical PP&E purchases are modest relative to revenue.
feasibility_checkpoints.scale_economics: Scale economics appear favorable because Services monetization is not capital intensive.
feasibility_checkpoints.guidance_alignment: No management target was used as proof; current disclosed data supports only a normal constraint.

step5_revenue_ceiling.reason: Asset-light exemption applies; no hard reinvestment ceiling is required.

validation_warnings.message: The Services synergy is capped until causality is reviewed.
